steps.title: Day 4: Writing Great Prompts
steps.body:
The single skill that separates AI operators from AI dabblers is prompt writing. A great prompt gets you exactly what you need on the first try. A bad prompt wastes 10 minutes of back-and-forth.

Today you'll learn the 4-part prompt formula that every professional AI operator uses.

The 4-part prompt formula:

1. ROLE — Tell the AI who to be ("You are an expert copywriter...")
2. CONTEXT — Give it the background it needs ("We're launching a productivity app for freelancers...")
3. TASK — Be specific about what you want ("Write 3 headline options for our landing page that...")
4. FORMAT — Define the output structure ("Each headline should be under 10 words, followed by a 2-sentence explanation")
highlights: ROLE — Tell the AI who to be
CONTEXT — Give it the background it needs
TASK — Be specific about what you want

steps.question: Which part of the 4-part formula tells the AI what kind of professional to act as?
feedback: Correct. ROLE sets the AI's persona — 'You are an expert negotiator' vs 'You are a kindergarten teacher' changes everything about how it responds.
CONTEXT gives background information, not the persona itself.
TASK says what to do, not who to be.
FORMAT defines structure — short, bulleted, JSON, etc.

steps.title: Before and After
steps.prompt:
❌ Bad: 'Write me a sales email'

✅ Good: 'You are a B2B sales expert. Our company sells project management software to mid-size tech companies. Write a cold outreach email to a VP of Engineering. Keep it under 150 words, friendly but professional tone, with a clear call-to-action to book a 15-minute demo.'

steps.question:
Is this a good prompt? 

'Make it better.'
feedback: Exactly. Without context, the AI has no idea what 'it' is or what 'better' means. Good catch.
This is actually a terrible prompt. 'It' is undefined and 'better' has no criteria. The AI will guess and waste your time.

steps.question: True or false: Longer prompts are always better prompts.
feedback: Right. Length doesn't equal quality. A clear 3-sentence prompt with role+task+format beats a rambling paragraph. Be specific, not verbose.
Not quite. A prompt should be as long as it needs to be — no more. Specificity matters, not word count.

fields.label: Context (what's the situation?)
fields.placeholder: e.g. Launching a new fitness app for busy parents

fields.label: Format (any output rules?)
fields.placeholder: e.g. Under 150 chars each, include emoji

questions.prompt: What's one task you do regularly that would benefit from a better prompt? Write the improved version.
questions.placeholder: I often ask AI to... but a better prompt would be...

steps.title: Day 4 Complete!
steps.body: You now have the 4-part prompt formula. This alone will save you hours every week. Tomorrow: building reusable prompt templates that you never have to rewrite.
